title: Equipment, Vehicle & Commercial Leasing
metaTitle: Leasing Solutions in BC — Equipment, Vehicle & Commercial
metaDescription: Leasing solutions for equipment, vehicles and commercial assets across BC. Through Dominion Lending Centres National's leasing partners.
intro: Through Dominion Lending Centres National's leasing partners, I can refer you to leasing solutions for vehicles, equipment and commercial assets — useful for self-employed clients and small businesses.

body.heading: When leasing beats buying
paragraphs: Leasing typically makes sense when: you want predictable monthly costs without large up-front capital outlay; you'll replace the asset on a fixed cycle (3–5 years for vehicles, 5–7 for equipment); the asset depreciates faster than you can sell it efficiently; or you want to preserve cash for higher-return uses elsewhere.
Leasing is generally not optimal for assets you'll keep long-term, where ownership equity accumulates value, or where you have ample cash and a low cost of capital.

whoItsFor: Are self-employed or run a small business
Need vehicles, equipment or commercial assets
Want to preserve cash for other uses

faqs.question: Does leasing affect my mortgage qualification?
faqs.answer: Yes — lease payments count as monthly debt obligations in your TDS calculation, the same way a loan payment would. Plan for this in your mortgage timeline.
